meta.title: Broken Cities or Civic Renewal?
meta.description: Just over two years ago, the citizens of Bell, California, discovered from an exposé in the Los Angeles Times that their city officials were among the highest paid in the nation, in spite of the city's underperforming economy and high unemployment rate. After some officials were arrested, the city was left with no functioning government and empty coffers.This might seem like the least favorable environment for encouraging civic engagement in public affairs, but that is not how Pepperdine Unive
